page: UpCloud HIMEM-16xCPU-384GB is a memory-optimized server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and the architecture is x86_64. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, translating to a high memory density of 24.0 GB per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 600 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng falls into the bottom 10%, while multi-core CPU performance reaches the middle 50% with a score of 12,131.25. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance across both small and large block reads and writes, with uncached large block reads reaching 104.49 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also perform in the average tier, with Redis handling 22.34 million operations per second. Large-scale LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with prompt processing for a 70B model rated as weak at 5.3 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers high resource density specifically for memory-bound workloads, though the shared CPU allocation and lower single-core performance represent a tradeoff for compute-heavy tasks. The instance is best suited for memory-intensive applications such as large databases, caching layers, in-memory data processing, and general-purpose workloads that require substantial RAM but do not demand sustained, high-frequency single-core CPU performance.
description: UpCloud HIMEM-16xCPU-384GB is a memory-optimized KVM-based server featuring 16 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing 24.0 GB per core, and includes a 600 GB local SSD. Benchmark data shows a performance tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is poor, falling in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations reside in the average tier. The server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-dense workloads that do not require dedicated or high-frequency single-core execution. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing workloads.
og_description: UpCloud HIMEM-16xCPU-384GB is a memory-optimized KVM server with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and a 600 GB local SSD, offering average multi-core performance but poor single-core speeds.

tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring high RAM-to-core ratios and local SSD storage for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
